description: Build a dense single-page analytics dashboard for 'MetricHub' at 1920px wide. It must contain, in this order: (1) a fixed top bar with the 'MetricHub' logo on the left, a centered search input, and a user avatar + 'Settings' link on the right; (2) a left sidebar (240px) with 6 nav items (Overview, Traffic, Revenue, Users, Reports, Settings) where 'Overview' is the active/highlighted item; (3) a main area with a row of FOUR equal-width KPI cards (Revenue $128,400 +12.3%, Users 24,108 +4.1%, Sessions 91,233 -2.0%, Conversion 3.4% +0.6%) each showing label, big number, and a colored delta (green for +, red for -); (4) below, TWO equal-width chart panels side by side ('Revenue over time' and 'Traffic sources') each at least 320px tall with a titled header and an inline-SVG chart placeholder; (5) below that, a full-width data table 'Top pages' with columns Page | Views | Avg time | Bounce and at least 6 rows. Responsive: at 375px the sidebar collapses (hidden or to a top hamburger), KPI cards stack to 1 column, and chart panels stack vertically.
requirements: A fixed top bar contains the logo (left), a centered search input, and avatar + Settings (right), all vertically centered
The left sidebar is ~240px wide and lists all six nav items with 'Overview' visibly highlighted as active
The four KPI cards are equal width, aligned in one row with uniform gaps, each showing label, number, and a delta
Positive deltas are green and the negative Sessions delta (-2.0%) is red
The two chart panels are equal width, side by side, each >=320px tall with a titled header and a visible chart placeholder
The 'Top pages' table shows all four column headers and at least 6 data rows with no cell text clipped
On desktop (1920px) nothing overflows horizontally and there is no unexpected horizontal scrollbar
At 375px width the four KPI cards stack into a single column
At 375px width the two chart panels stack vertically (not side by side)
At 375px the sidebar is collapsed/hidden (replaced by a hamburger or removed), not overlapping the content
All numeric values listed are present exactly as given
Text and background colors maintain legible contrast throughout
expected_issues: KPI cards drift to unequal widths or wrap awkwardly
Sidebar overlaps main content or does not collapse on mobile
Charts overflow their panels; table cells clipped

description: Build a cloud-hosting pricing page for 'NimbusCloud' at 1920px wide with BOTH a 3-tier card row AND a dense feature comparison table. Tiers: Starter $0/mo, Pro $29/mo (mark 'Most popular' with a colored top border), Business $99/mo. Each card lists price, a one-line tagline, a primary CTA button, and 4 bullet features. Below the cards, a comparison table with a left feature-name column and the three plan columns, at least 12 feature rows (e.g. 'Projects', 'Bandwidth', 'Build minutes', 'Team seats', 'Custom domains', 'SSL', 'Analytics', 'Priority support', 'SSO', 'Audit logs', 'SLA', 'Uptime') using checkmarks/dashes or values per cell. A FAQ section with 5 question rows below. Responsive: at 375px the three pricing cards stack vertically and the comparison table becomes horizontally scrollable within its own container (not the whole page).
requirements: Three pricing tier cards (Starter $0, Pro $29, Business $99) are equal width, top-aligned, in one row
The Pro card is marked 'Most popular' with a colored top border at least 4px
Each card shows price, tagline, a CTA button, and exactly four feature bullets
The comparison table has a feature-name column plus three plan columns, all aligned
The comparison table has at least 12 feature rows with a value or check/dash in every cell (no empty unaligned cells)
The FAQ section lists exactly five question rows
On desktop nothing overflows horizontally; the table fits within the page width
At 375px the three pricing cards stack into a single column
At 375px the comparison table scrolls horizontally within its own container, not forcing the whole page to scroll sideways
All listed prices ($0, $29, $99) appear exactly
A consistent accent color is reused across the Pro highlight, CTAs, and table checkmarks
expected_issues: Comparison table overflows the page width on mobile
Pricing cards unequal height/width or misaligned tops
Empty comparison cells break the column grid

description: Build a three-column documentation page for an open-source library 'querykit' at 1920px wide: (1) a LEFT sidebar (260px) with a nav tree of 3 sections each with 3-4 sub-links, current page 'Quickstart' highlighted; (2) a CENTER content column (max 760px, centered in remaining space) with an H1 'Quickstart', intro paragraph, an 'Installation' H2 followed by a code block 'npm install querykit', a 'Usage' H2 with a second multi-line code block, and a note/callout box; (3) a RIGHT 'On this page' table-of-contents (200px) listing the in-page headings (Installation, Usage, Notes) with the first one highlighted. A top bar spans all three columns with logo + version selector + GitHub link. Responsive: at 375px both side columns hide and only the center content (full width) and a top hamburger remain.
requirements: A top bar spans the full width with the logo, a version selector, and a GitHub link
The left sidebar (~260px) shows a nav tree of three sections with sub-links and 'Quickstart' highlighted
The center content column is constrained (max ~760px) and not full-bleed on desktop
Both code blocks are monospaced, visually distinct (background/border), and do not overflow their container
The right 'On this page' TOC lists Installation, Usage, Notes with the first highlighted
The three columns are vertically aligned at the same top and do not overlap
On desktop nothing overflows horizontally
At 375px both the left sidebar and right TOC are hidden, leaving the center content full width
At 375px a hamburger or menu affordance replaces the sidebar without overlapping content
The callout/note box is visually distinct from body text and fully contained
Heading hierarchy is visually clear (H1 larger/heavier than H2)
expected_issues: Three-column layout collapses incorrectly or columns overlap
Center column runs full-bleed (no max width)
Side columns still present / overlapping on mobile

description: Build a conference schedule page for 'DevConf 2026' at 1920px wide as a multi-track timetable. A header shows the conference name, date 'June 15, 2026', and a 3-tab track filter (All, Frontend, Backend). The schedule is a grid: a left time column (9:00 to 13:00 in 30-min rows, 9 rows) and THREE track columns (Main Stage, Frontend, Backend) of equal width. At least 8 session blocks are placed in the grid, each showing a title, speaker, and a colored tag for its track; at least one session spans two time rows (a 60-min talk) and the 10:00 keynote spans all three track columns. Below the grid, a legend mapping the three track colors. Responsive: at 375px the three track columns stack into a single vertical list grouped by time.
requirements: A header shows 'DevConf 2026', the date 'June 15, 2026', and a three-tab track filter
A left time column lists 9:00 through 13:00 in 30-minute increments (9 rows)
There are three equal-width track columns labeled Main Stage, Frontend, Backend, aligned to the time rows
At least eight session blocks appear, each with a title, a speaker, and a track-colored tag
At least one session block visibly spans two 30-minute rows
The 10:00 keynote spans the full width of all three track columns
A legend maps each of the three track colors to its track name
Session blocks align to their time-row boundaries without overlapping adjacent blocks
On desktop nothing overflows horizontally
At 375px the track columns stack into a single time-ordered vertical list
Track tag colors are used consistently between the blocks and the legend
expected_issues: Session blocks misaligned to the time grid or overlapping
Row-spanning / column-spanning sessions break the grid
Grid does not linearize on mobile

description: Build a code-hosting repository page (GitHub-style) for 'acme/querykit' at 1920px wide. Top: a repo header with owner/name breadcrumb, a description, and a row of three stat buttons (Star 1.2k, Fork 184, Watch 56). Below, a two-column layout: LEFT (main, ~70%) a file-browser toolbar ('main' branch selector, '12 commits' link) above a file list table with at least 7 rows (mix of folders and files: src/, tests/, README.md, package.json, LICENSE, .gitignore, tsconfig.json) each with name, last-commit message, and relative time; below the file list a rendered README card with an H1 and a paragraph. RIGHT (sidebar, ~30%) an 'About' panel with the description, a topics row of 4 tag chips, and a 'Releases' line; below it a language bar (a thin horizontal stacked bar) with a legend (TypeScript 78%, CSS 14%, JavaScript 8%). Responsive: at 375px the sidebar drops below the main column (single column).
requirements: A repo header shows the owner/name breadcrumb, a description, and three stat buttons (Star 1.2k, Fork 184, Watch 56)
The main column is roughly 70% width and the About sidebar roughly 30%, side by side and top-aligned
A file-list table shows at least 7 rows, each with a name, a last-commit message, and a relative time
Folder rows are visually distinguishable from file rows (icon or style)
A README card below the file list shows an H1 heading and a paragraph
The About sidebar shows the description, exactly four topic chips, and a Releases line
A stacked language bar appears with a legend listing TypeScript 78%, CSS 14%, JavaScript 8%
The language-bar segment widths are proportional to the listed percentages
On desktop nothing overflows horizontally; file-list cells are not clipped
At 375px the About sidebar moves below the main column (single column layout)
The accent color is consistent across links, chips, and the active branch selector
expected_issues: File list cells (long commit messages) overflow or clip
Two-column layout does not stack on mobile
Topic chips wrap untidily / unequal styling

description: Build a service status page for 'NimbusCloud Status' at 1920px wide. Top: an overall banner 'All Systems Operational' with a green indicator. Below, a 'Current status' section listing SEVEN services (API, Dashboard, CDN, Database, Auth, Webhooks, Email) each as a row with the service name (left) and a status pill (right): five 'Operational' (green), one 'Degraded Performance' (amber, for CDN), one 'Partial Outage' (red, for Webhooks). Below, a 'Uptime (90 days)' section showing, for each of the seven services, a row of 90 thin vertical bars (mostly green with a few amber/red) and a '99.98%' figure at the right. Below, an 'Incident history' timeline with 3 dated incident entries, each with a title, a colored severity dot, and a short description. Responsive: at 375px the service name and status pill stack within each row and the 90-bar strips remain on one line (may shrink).
requirements: An overall banner reads 'All Systems Operational' with a green status indicator
Exactly seven services are listed, each with a name and a status pill
Five services show green 'Operational', CDN shows amber 'Degraded Performance', Webhooks shows red 'Partial Outage'
Each of the seven services has an uptime strip of 90 thin vertical bars
Each uptime strip shows a percentage figure (e.g. 99.98%) aligned to its right
The incident history lists exactly three dated entries, each with a title, a severity dot, and a description
Status pill colors (green/amber/red) are used consistently between the service list and uptime bars
On desktop the seven rows are left-aligned and the pills are right-aligned in a consistent column
Nothing overflows horizontally on desktop
At 375px each service row stacks the name above its pill, and the 90-bar strip stays on a single line
The seven service rows share consistent height and vertical rhythm
expected_issues: 90-bar uptime strips wrap to multiple lines or overflow
Rows of uneven height / misaligned pills

description: Build an e-commerce product detail page for 'Aero Running Shoe' at 1920px wide. Two-column hero: LEFT a gallery with one large main image placeholder and a row of 4 thumbnail placeholders below it; RIGHT a product info panel with title, a star rating (4.5 with '128 reviews'), price '$139.00', a size selector (chips 7,8,9,10,11), a quantity stepper, and a full-width 'Add to cart' button. Below the hero, a specifications table (at least 8 rows: Weight, Drop, Upper, Midsole, Outsole, Use, Colorway, SKU). Below that, a reviews section with 3 review cards (author, stars, text). Below that, a 'You may also like' row of 4 equal-width related-product cards. A sticky mini add-to-cart bar appears pinned to the bottom showing the product name and price. Responsive: at 375px the hero stacks (gallery above info), the related row becomes a horizontal scroller, and the sticky bar remains pinned.
requirements: The hero is two columns on desktop: gallery (left) and product info (right), top-aligned
The gallery shows one large image placeholder and exactly four thumbnail placeholders in a row
The info panel shows title, a 4.5 star rating with '128 reviews', price '$139.00', size chips (7-11), a quantity stepper, and a full-width Add to cart button
The specifications table has at least 8 rows, each with a label and a value, no cell clipped
The reviews section shows exactly three review cards, each with author, stars, and text
The 'You may also like' row shows four equal-width related-product cards with uniform gaps
A sticky bar pinned to the bottom shows the product name and the price $139.00
On desktop nothing overflows horizontally and the sticky bar does not cover essential hero content
At 375px the hero stacks vertically (gallery above info)
At 375px the related-products row becomes a horizontal scroller (cards do not shrink illegibly or wrap messily)
The price '$139.00' appears identically in the info panel and the sticky bar
expected_issues: Sticky bar overlaps content or disappears
Related cards unequal width / wrap on desktop
Hero does not stack on mobile

description: Build a newspaper-style homepage for 'The Daily Ledger' at 1920px wide with a dense multi-column grid. Masthead: centered title 'The Daily Ledger', a date line 'Monday, June 1, 2026', and a horizontal nav of 6 sections (World, Business, Tech, Science, Culture, Opinion). Below, a 12-column-style article grid: a large lead story spanning the left two-thirds (headline, byline, image placeholder, 3-line dek), a right column with 3 stacked secondary stories (headline + 1-line summary, separated by hairlines), and below the fold a row of 4 equal-width 'More headlines' cards. A thin section divider rule separates blocks. Responsive: at 375px everything collapses to a single column in reading order (lead, then secondaries, then the 4 cards).
requirements: A centered masthead shows 'The Daily Ledger', the date line, and a 6-section horizontal nav
A lead story occupies the left two-thirds with a headline, byline, image placeholder, and a multi-line dek
A right column holds exactly three stacked secondary stories separated by hairline rules
Below the fold, four equal-width 'More headlines' cards appear in one row with uniform gaps
The lead column and the right column are top-aligned and do not overlap
Hairline/divider rules are consistent in weight and color (repetition)
Headline typography clearly outranks body/summary text (contrast/hierarchy)
On desktop nothing overflows horizontally
At 375px the entire layout collapses to a single column in the order lead, secondaries, then the four cards
The six nav sections are evenly spaced and aligned on one row on desktop
All six section names and the date line appear exactly as specified
expected_issues: Multi-column grid misaligns; lead and sidebar overlap
Layout does not linearize correctly on mobile

description: Build an account settings page for 'Workspace' at 1920px wide using a two-pane layout: LEFT a settings nav (220px) with 5 items (Profile, Account, Notifications, Billing, Security), 'Notifications' active. RIGHT a content pane showing the Notifications settings as THREE grouped sections ('Email', 'Push', 'SMS'), each a card with a header and 3-4 labeled toggle rows (label + description on the left, an on/off toggle switch on the right) - at least 10 toggle rows total, with a clear mix of on and off states. A sticky footer action bar within the content pane has 'Cancel' and a primary 'Save changes' button right-aligned. Responsive: at 375px the left nav becomes a horizontal scrollable tab strip on top and the toggle rows stack label-above-control.
requirements: A left settings nav (~220px) lists all five items with 'Notifications' highlighted as active
The content pane shows three grouped cards: Email, Push, SMS
There are at least 10 toggle rows total across the three cards
Each toggle row has a left-aligned label (+description) and a right-aligned on/off switch in a consistent column
Both on and off toggle states are visually distinct and both appear among the rows
A sticky action bar shows 'Cancel' and a primary 'Save changes' button, right-aligned
The toggle switches all share one consistent style (repetition)
On desktop the toggle controls align in a single right-hand column across all rows
Nothing overflows horizontally on desktop
At 375px the left nav becomes a top tab strip and toggle rows stack the control below the label
The primary 'Save changes' button is visually higher-contrast than 'Cancel'
expected_issues: Toggle controls not aligned in a consistent column
Action bar overlaps last toggle rows
Nav does not transform on mobile

description: Build an arXiv-style paper search-results page for 'OpenPapers' at 1920px wide. Top: a search bar pre-filled with 'transformer efficiency' and a results count '1-10 of 3,482'. Two-column layout: LEFT a filters sidebar (260px) with grouped facets ('Year' with 4 checkboxes 2026-2023, 'Category' with 4 checkboxes, 'Sort by' radio group) and an 'Apply' button. RIGHT a results list of at least 6 paper entries, each showing a title (link style), an author line, a venue+year line, a 2-line abstract snippet, and a row of 3 small tag chips. A pagination control (Prev, 1 2 3 ... 349, Next) sits at the bottom of the results. Responsive: at 375px the filters sidebar collapses into a 'Filters' button/disclosure above the results and entries go full width.
requirements: A search bar is pre-filled with 'transformer efficiency' and a results count '1-10 of 3,482' is shown
A left filters sidebar (~260px) shows Year (4 checkboxes), Category (4 checkboxes), and a Sort-by radio group, plus an Apply button
The results list shows at least 6 paper entries
Each paper entry has a title, an author line, a venue+year line, a 2-line abstract snippet, and exactly three tag chips
Abstract snippets are truncated to about two lines (not full paragraphs) and do not overflow
A pagination control with Prev/Next and numbered pages appears at the bottom of the results
The sidebar and results list are top-aligned and do not overlap on desktop
Tag chips share a consistent style across all entries (repetition)
On desktop nothing overflows horizontally
At 375px the filters collapse into a disclosure/button above the results and entries become full width
Title links use a consistent accent color distinct from body text
expected_issues: Abstract snippets not clamped (overflow / uneven entry heights)
Sidebar overlaps results or does not collapse on mobile

description: Build a project board (Kanban) for 'ShipIt' at 1920px wide. A top bar shows the board name, a member-avatars stack, and a 'New task' button. Below, FOUR equal-width columns (Backlog, In Progress, Review, Done) with uniform gutters, each with a column header showing the title and a count badge, and a vertical stack of task cards. Distribute at least 14 task cards across the columns (Backlog 5, In Progress 4, Review 3, Done 2); each card shows a title, a small colored label chip, an assignee avatar, and a due date. Responsive: at 375px the four columns stack into one vertical column in order, each column's cards beneath its header.
requirements: Exactly four columns (Backlog, In Progress, Review, Done) of equal width with uniform gutters between them
The four column headers share the same top edge and each shows a title plus a count badge
At least 14 task cards total are distributed across the columns
Every task card shows a title, a colored label chip, an assignee avatar, and a due date, none clipped
All cards within a column are equal width and left/right aligned to their column
No card overlaps another card or column header
On desktop nothing overflows horizontally (no horizontal scrollbar)
At 375px the four columns stack vertically in order with each column's cards under its header
Card label chips share one consistent style across all cards (repetition)
Column count badges match the number of cards shown in each column
expected_issues: No horizontal scroll handling on mobile
Count badges inconsistent with card counts

description: Build a three-pane email client 'Mailbox' at 1920px wide: LEFT folder rail (220px) with 6 folders (Inbox 12, Starred, Sent, Drafts 3, Archive, Trash) where Inbox is active and unread counts are right-aligned badges; CENTER message list (380px) with at least 8 message rows each showing sender, subject, a one-line snippet, and a time, with unread rows visually bolder; RIGHT reading pane (remaining width) showing the selected message: subject header, sender + avatar + time, a toolbar (Reply, Forward, Archive, Delete), and a body of several paragraphs. The three panes share the same top and bottom edges. Responsive: at 375px only the message list shows (folder rail becomes a hamburger, reading pane hidden until selection).
requirements: Three panes (folder rail, message list, reading pane) sit side by side sharing the same top and bottom edge
The folder rail lists all six folders with Inbox active and unread counts as right-aligned badges
The message list shows at least 8 rows, each with sender, subject, snippet, and time
Unread message rows are visually distinct (bolder) from read rows
The reading pane shows a subject header, sender/avatar/time, a toolbar with at least four actions, and a multi-paragraph body
The folder unread badges are right-aligned in a consistent column
No pane content overlaps another pane; snippets are truncated, not overflowing
On desktop nothing overflows horizontally
At 375px the layout collapses to the message list with the folder rail behind a hamburger and the reading pane hidden
Toolbar action buttons share a consistent style

description: Build a month-view calendar for 'June 2026' at 1920px wide. A header shows 'June 2026', prev/next arrows, and a 'Today' button. Below, a 7-column grid (Sun..Sat column headers) with 5 week rows of equal-width, equal-height day cells. Fill the dates 1-30 in the correct positions (June 1 2026 is a Monday). Place at least 6 event chips inside day cells (e.g. '10:00 Standup', '14:00 Review'), with one day showing '+2 more'. Today (e.g. the 1st) is highlighted. Responsive: at 375px the calendar becomes a vertical agenda list of only the days that have events, each day a row with its event chips.
requirements: A header shows 'June 2026', prev/next controls, and a Today button
Seven equal-width day-of-week column headers (Sun through Sat) are aligned over the grid
The grid has 5 week rows of equal-height, equal-width day cells (35 cells)
Dates 1-30 appear once each in the correct cells, with June 1 in the Monday column
At least six event chips appear inside day cells, and one cell shows a '+N more' overflow indicator
The 'today' cell is visually highlighted distinctly from the others
Day cells are uniformly sized and the grid lines/edges align across all rows and columns
Event chips do not overflow their day cell (they clamp or show '+N more')
On desktop nothing overflows horizontally
At 375px the calendar becomes a vertical agenda list of event-bearing days, not a squeezed 7-column grid
expected_issues: Day cells unequal size / grid misaligned

description: Build a messaging app 'Pulse' at 1920px wide: LEFT conversation list (320px) with a search box and at least 6 conversation rows (avatar, name, last-message snippet, time, and some with an unread count badge); RIGHT a chat thread with a header (contact name + avatar + online dot), a scrollable message area showing at least 8 alternating bubbles (incoming left-aligned, outgoing right-aligned, with timestamps), and a fixed composer at the bottom (text input + send button). The two panes share the same top and bottom edges. Responsive: at 375px only the chat thread shows with a back arrow; the conversation list is hidden.
requirements: Two panes (conversation list and chat thread) share the same top and bottom edge
The conversation list has a search box and at least 6 rows, each with avatar, name, snippet, and time
At least some conversations show an unread count badge, right-aligned
The chat thread has a header with contact name, avatar, and an online indicator
At least 8 message bubbles appear: incoming bubbles left-aligned, outgoing bubbles right-aligned
Each message bubble has a timestamp and does not overflow the thread width
A composer (input + send button) is pinned to the bottom of the thread and does not overlap messages
Incoming vs outgoing bubbles use consistent, distinct styling (repetition + contrast)
On desktop nothing overflows horizontally
At 375px only the chat thread is shown (with a back affordance) and the conversation list is hidden

description: Build a printable invoice page for 'Acme Co.' at 1920px wide, centered in an A4-like sheet (max ~900px). Header: company name + logo placeholder on the left, 'INVOICE #2026-0042' and issue/due dates on the right. Below, two address blocks side by side (From / Bill To) of equal width. Then a line-items table with columns Description | Qty | Unit price | Amount and at least 6 rows, all numbers right-aligned. Below the table, a totals block right-aligned with Subtotal $4,200.00, Tax (8%) $336.00, and a bold Total $4,536.00. A footer note spans the bottom. Responsive: at 375px the From/Bill To blocks stack and the line-items table scrolls horizontally within its container.
requirements: The invoice sheet is centered with a constrained max width (not full-bleed)
The header shows the company (left) and 'INVOICE #2026-0042' with dates (right), top-aligned
The From and Bill To address blocks are equal width and side by side
The line-items table has all four columns and at least 6 rows with no clipped cells
All Qty, Unit price, and Amount values are right-aligned in their columns
The totals block is right-aligned and shows Subtotal $4,200.00, Tax (8%) $336.00, and a bold Total $4,536.00
The three total figures appear exactly as specified and Total is visually emphasized
On desktop nothing overflows horizontally
At 375px the From/Bill To blocks stack vertically and the table scrolls within its own container
Consistent typographic hierarchy (INVOICE heading dominates; labels lighter than values)
expected_issues: Table breaks page width on mobile

description: Build a social profile page for '@maya.codes' at 1920px wide. A full-width cover banner, with a circular avatar overlapping its bottom edge, the display name 'Maya Chen', handle, and a bio line. A stats row of THREE equal-width items (Posts 248, Followers 12.4k, Following 386) with a 'Follow' button. A tab bar (Posts, Media, Likes) with Posts active. Below, a responsive post grid of at least 9 equal-size square thumbnail placeholders in 3 columns with uniform gaps. Responsive: at 375px the stats row stays in one line (smaller), and the post grid becomes 1 column (or stays a tight grid) without horizontal overflow.
requirements: A full-width cover banner with a circular avatar overlapping its bottom edge
The display name, handle, and a bio line are present and legible over/under the banner
A stats row shows three equal-width items (Posts 248, Followers 12.4k, Following 386) plus a Follow button
A tab bar shows Posts, Media, Likes with Posts visibly active
The post grid shows at least 9 equal-size square thumbnails in 3 columns with uniform gaps
The avatar is circular and does not overlap the name/stats text
Thumbnails are uniformly sized and aligned in a clean grid (no ragged last row gaps misaligning the columns)
On desktop nothing overflows horizontally
At 375px the layout has no horizontal overflow and the stats row remains on one line
A single accent color is reused for the active tab, the Follow button, and links
expected_issues: Stats row wraps badly on mobile

description: Build a checkout page for 'Checkout' at 1920px wide as a two-column layout: LEFT (60%) a form with three labeled sections (Contact, Shipping address with a 2x2 grid of fields, Payment with card-number/expiry/CVC fields) using aligned labels and inputs; RIGHT (40%) an order summary card that is sticky on scroll, listing 3 line items (name, qty, price), a promo-code input, and Subtotal $172.00 / Shipping $8.00 / Total $180.00, plus a full-width 'Pay $180.00' button. Responsive: at 375px the order summary moves below the form (not sticky), and the 2x2 address grid becomes single-column.
requirements: A two-column layout: form (~60%) on the left and order summary (~40%) on the right, top-aligned
The form has three clearly grouped sections (Contact, Shipping, Payment)
Form fields have aligned labels and inputs; the shipping address uses a 2x2 field grid that lines up
The order summary lists three line items each with name, qty, and price
The order summary shows Subtotal $172.00, Shipping $8.00, Total $180.00 and a 'Pay $180.00' button
The Total $180.00 and the Pay button amount match exactly
The order summary is sticky on desktop scroll and does not overlap the form
On desktop nothing overflows horizontally
At 375px the order summary moves below the form and the 2x2 address grid becomes a single column
Inputs share a consistent style and the Pay button is the highest-contrast action
expected_issues: Sticky summary overlaps form or jumps
2x2 grid does not collapse on mobile

description: Build a product comparison / spec-sheet page for 'Compare Phones' at 1920px wide comparing THREE phones side by side. A header row has an empty top-left cell then three equal-width phone columns, each with an image placeholder, model name, and price (Aero $799, Aero Pro $999, Aero Max $1199). Below, a dense specification table with a left attribute column and the three phone columns, at least 14 attribute rows (Display, Resolution, Chip, RAM, Storage, Rear camera, Front camera, Battery, Charging, Weight, Water resistance, Biometrics, Colors, OS) with a value in every cell. Alternating row shading. A sticky compare header keeps the phone names visible on scroll. Responsive: at 375px it shows one phone column at a time with a selector, or the table scrolls horizontally within its container.
requirements: A header row aligns three equal-width phone columns over the spec table, each with image, name, and price
The three prices appear exactly (Aero $799, Aero Pro $999, Aero Max $1199)
The spec table has a left attribute column plus the three phone columns, all aligned
There are at least 14 attribute rows with a value in every cell (no empty unaligned cells)
Alternating rows are shaded consistently (repetition) and no cell text is clipped
The phone-name header stays visible on scroll (sticky) and stays aligned to its columns
On desktop nothing overflows horizontally; the table fits the page width
At 375px the comparison remains usable (single-column selector or horizontally scrollable table within its container), not a squashed 4-column grid
Column widths for the three phones are equal
The price/header typography outranks the spec values (hierarchy)

description: Build an image gallery page 'Shots' at 1920px wide. A header with the title and a filter bar of 5 category pills (All active, Nature, Urban, People, Abstract). Below, a masonry-style grid of at least 12 image-placeholder cards arranged in 4 columns; cards have varying heights but the COLUMNS stay equal width with uniform gutters and the cards pack without large ragged gaps. Each card has a hover-caption bar with a title and a small heart/like count. A 'Load more' button is centered at the bottom. Responsive: at 375px the grid becomes a single column and the filter pills become a horizontally scrollable row.
requirements: A filter bar shows five category pills with 'All' active
The grid arranges at least 12 cards in 4 equal-width columns with uniform gutters
Card heights may vary (masonry) but the four columns remain equal width and aligned
Cards pack without large ragged vertical gaps between columns
Each card has a caption bar with a title and a like count, not clipped
A centered 'Load more' button appears below the grid
Filter pills share one consistent style with the active pill visually distinct
On desktop nothing overflows horizontally
At 375px the grid becomes a single column and the filter pills scroll horizontally in their own row
Image placeholders maintain consistent styling/spacing (repetition)
expected_issues: Filter bar wraps messily on mobile

description: Build a long marketing landing page for a SaaS 'FlowState' at 1920px wide with many aligned sections stacked vertically: (1) sticky top nav (logo left; links Product, Pricing, Docs, Blog center; 'Sign in' + 'Get started' right); (2) hero (centered headline, subhead, two CTAs, a product screenshot placeholder); (3) a logos strip of 5 evenly-spaced grayscale customer-logo placeholders; (4) a 3-column features row (icon + title + 2-line description), equal width; (5) a 2-column 'how it works' alternating row (text + image); (6) a testimonial block (quote, author, avatar); (7) a 3-tier pricing teaser (Free, Pro, Team) equal-width cards; (8) an FAQ of 4 items; (9) a footer with 4 equal-width link columns and a bottom legal bar. Responsive: at 375px the nav collapses to a hamburger and every multi-column section becomes a single column.
requirements: A sticky top nav has the logo (left), centered links, and right-side auth buttons, all vertically centered
The hero has a centered headline, subhead, two CTA buttons, and a screenshot placeholder
The logos strip shows 5 evenly-spaced logo placeholders aligned on one row
The features row has 3 equal-width columns (icon + title + description) with uniform gutters
The pricing teaser shows 3 equal-width cards (Free, Pro, Team) top-aligned
The footer has 4 equal-width link columns plus a bottom legal bar
All major sections are full-width bands that align to a consistent content max-width / left edge (repetition)
On desktop nothing overflows horizontally and no two sections overlap vertically
At 375px the nav collapses to a hamburger
At 375px every multi-column section (features, how-it-works, pricing, footer) becomes a single column
One consistent accent color is reused across CTAs, active nav, and pricing highlight
expected_issues: Section content widths inconsistent (not aligned to a common grid)
Sections overlap / sticky nav covers hero
Multi-column sections don't collapse on mobile
